reforms.description: The government’s Growth and Innovation Framework emphasizes the need to strengthen innovation, which is crucial for enhancing productivity and output. However, it suggests that innovation policies could be streamlined and made more coherent to better support this goal.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening innovation policies is expected to enhance productivity and economic output, contributing positively to growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has highlighted the importance of strengthening innovation through its Growth and Innovation Framework.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the coherence and effectiveness of existing innovation policies, which is significant for improving overall economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation policies could be streamlined and made more coherent.

reforms.description: The government has announced a more neutral approach to depreciation allowances for capital equipment used in R&D, which aims to encourage business R&D expenditures that are currently below the OECD average.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the tax treatment of R&D investments, the reform is expected to stimulate private sector innovation and increase overall R&D spending.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has chosen to deliver public support for private innovation through a wide range of grants and subsidies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment in tax policy aimed at enhancing R&D investment, which is crucial for improving innovation performance.
reforms.source_quote: The more neutral approach announced in the Budget is welcome.

reforms.description: The government has signaled its intention to streamline and better coordinate public support for private innovation through various grants and subsidies, aiming to improve policy coherence.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing programme clutter and enhancing coordination, the reform is expected to make public support for innovation more effective and impactful.
reforms.status_evidence: These could be streamlined, better co-ordinated and carefully evaluated to improve policy coherence.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the effectiveness of existing innovation support mechanisms, which is crucial for fostering a more innovative economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government has chosen to deliver public support for private innovation through a wide range of grants and subsidies.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government allocated NZD24 million over four years for the Broadband Challenge, aimed at promoting high-speed broadband capacity for regional centres and small communities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing broadband infrastructure is expected to improve connectivity, stimulate economic activity, and support innovation in various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government provided funding as part of its Digital Strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding represents a significant investment in broadband infrastructure, which is crucial for enhancing digital connectivity and supporting economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The 2005 Budget provided NZD24 million over four years for the Broadband Challenge.

reforms.description: The Growth and Innovation Pilots provide funding for tertiary education organisations to deepen linkages with the biotechnology, design, and ICT sectors, aiming to enhance collaboration between public research institutions and private firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration between academia and industry, facilitating the transfer of knowledge and technology to the market.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been established to support collaboration between sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant effort to improve collaboration between public and private sectors, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Examples are research consortia established between private firms, Crown Research Institutes (CRIs) and universities, the Growth and Innovation Pilots that provide funding for tertiary education organisations to deepen linkages with the biotechnology, design, and ICT sectors.

reforms.description: The Partnerships for Excellence Programme aims to establish joint university-industry research centres, fostering collaboration and enhancing the commercialization of research outputs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the commercialization of research by creating direct links between universities and industry, enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been initiated to promote joint research efforts.
reforms.importance_rationale: A significant initiative aimed at enhancing university-industry collaboration, which is essential for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Partnerships for Excellence Programme to establish joint university-industry research centres.

reforms.description: The government’s Statement of Tertiary Education Priorities aims to redirect spending into courses that are more relevant to the labor market and eliminate low-quality courses, emphasizing the differentiation of roles among educational institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning educational offerings with labor market needs, the reform is expected to enhance the employability of graduates, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has released the Statement of Tertiary Education Priorities, indicating its commitment to these changes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the funding and focus of tertiary education, aiming to improve the quality and relevance of education in New Zealand, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s Statement of Tertiary Education Priorities...includes several important features: a sharper differentiation of roles between universities, polytechnics and wananga...

reforms.description: The Working for Families package aims to improve income adequacy and work incentives for low- and middle-income families by providing larger income transfers and reducing the costs of childcare.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing financial support and reducing childcare costs, the package is expected to increase labor supply and improve overall economic participation.
reforms.status_evidence: The package is set to be fully implemented by April 2007.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the income support system, aiming to enhance work incentives and support for families, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Working for Families package has three objectives: making work pay, improving income adequacy, and achieving a social assistance system that supports people into work.

reforms.description: The government announced changes to the science funding system, increasing the non-contestable component for Crown Research Institutes (CRIs) to enhance their research capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for CRIs is expected to enhance research output and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Changes to the science funding system with an increase in the non-contestable component for CRIs were announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to the funding structure for CRIs, which is crucial for enhancing their research capabilities and overall innovation output.
reforms.source_quote: Changes to the science funding system with an increase in the non-contestable component for CRIs were announced.

reforms.description: The government released its Digital Strategy to enhance the uptake of ICT across various sectors, including firms and educational institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting ICT uptake, the strategy aims to improve productivity and innovation across sectors, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government released its Digital Strategy to bring together a range of initiatives to increase the uptake of ICT.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Digital Strategy is a significant initiative aimed at enhancing ICT adoption, which is critical for modernizing the economy and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government released its Digital Strategy to bring together a range of initiatives to increase the uptake of ICT across firms, government and education.

reforms.description: The government announced the expansion of the Technology for Industry Fellowships scheme to include an international component, promoting technology transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This expansion is expected to enhance collaboration between academia and industry, facilitating the transfer of knowledge and technology.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced expansion of the Technology for Industry Fellowships scheme to include an international component.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of this scheme represents a significant enhancement of an existing program aimed at fostering technology transfer and collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced expansion of the Technology for Industry Fellowships scheme – aimed at promoting technology transfer by seconding students, experts, and recent graduates to work on science and technology projects in firms – to include an international component.

reforms.description: The venture capital tax reforms removed tax barriers to New Zealand companies attracting private equity and venture capital from institutional investors in specific countries, facilitating investment in innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing tax barriers, the reforms encourage investment in startups and innovative firms, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were enacted to facilitate venture capital investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly enhance the investment landscape for innovative firms, promoting growth in the venture capital sector.
reforms.source_quote: The venture capital tax reforms removed tax barriers to NZ companies attracting private equity and venture capital from institutional investors in specific countries.

reforms.description: The government announced plans to remove taxation on gains made from the sale of domestic shares by collective investment vehicles, aiming to create a more efficient allocation of capital for firms.
reforms.component_name: Removal of Capital Gains Tax on Domestic Shares
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By removing this tax, the reform is expected to enhance investment flows into firms, thereby supporting productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced this measure in the 2005 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant distortion in the capital market, potentially leading to improved investment patterns, though it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the tax system.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced in the 2005 Budget that it would remove taxation on the gains made on the sale of domestic shares by collective investment vehicles.

reforms.description: Legislation was introduced to reform tax depreciation rates to better align them with economic depreciation, particularly for short-lived assets, which is expected to encourage more investment in machinery and equipment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning tax depreciation with economic realities, the reform is expected to incentivize investment in productive assets, thereby enhancing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Legislation was introduced to Parliament on Budget day (19 May 2005).
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical aspect of tax policy that affects investment decisions, with potential long-term benefits for productivity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Legislation was introduced to Parliament on Budget day (19 May 2005) to reform the calculation of tax depreciation rates.

reforms.description: The government has emphasized strengthening innovation as a key element of its growth strategy within the Growth and Innovation Framework, aiming to boost productivity through increased R&D expenditure and innovation activities among firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding and innovation activities are expected to enhance productivity and efficiency in firms, leading to higher economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Strengthening innovation has been a key element of the government’s growth strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy focus towards innovation, which is crucial for enhancing productivity and economic growth in New Zealand.
reforms.source_quote: Strengthening innovation has been a key element of the government’s growth strategy within the Growth and Innovation Framework.

reforms.description: The government has proposed to progressively increase the non-contestable proportion of public funding for research institutes, aiming to provide more stable long-term funding while ensuring competition among research providers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Stable funding can enhance research capabilities, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Minister for Research, Science and Technology has proposed this change.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment in funding strategy, aiming to balance stability and competition in research funding.
reforms.source_quote: The Minister for Research, Science and Technology has proposed that the non-contestable proportion be progressively increased in the coming years.

reforms.description: The government has announced changes to the venture capital market, including tax exemptions for non-residents investing in the NZVenture Investment Fund and improvements to tax deductibility of R&D expenditure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These changes are expected to enhance investment in high-risk projects, facilitating innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced it will exempt non-residents who invest in the VIF from tax on the profits.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to stimulate the venture capital market, which is crucial for funding innovative startups.
reforms.source_quote: The government has announced it will exempt non-residents who invest in the VIF from tax on the profits when they later sell their shares.

reforms.description: The government has initiated systematic evaluations of programmes to support innovation, aiming to enhance the effectiveness and coherence of public support for research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By evaluating innovation programmes, the government can identify effective strategies and improve resource allocation, ultimately fostering a more innovative economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has already embarked on efforts to address these problems.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards improving the effectiveness of public innovation support, which is crucial for enhancing overall productivity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Undertaking systematic evaluations of programmes to support innovation.

reforms.description: The government is improving coordination among agencies responsible for public support, which is expected to enhance the coherence of policies related to innovation funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Better coordination among agencies can lead to more effective use of resources and improved outcomes for innovation policies, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Improving coordination among agencies responsible for the delivery of public support.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to streamline public support mechanisms, which can enhance the overall effectiveness of innovation policies.
reforms.source_quote: Improving coordination among agencies responsible for the delivery of public support and setting guidelines for the provision of grants.
